title: 21-22 Math Dept Ann Program Review (Part 1)
text:
ANNUAL PROGRAM REVIEW FOR DEPARTMENT OF MATHEMATICS
2021-2022
by
Wenli Chang & Shirley Louie
PART 1: Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
The Department of Mathematics at College of the Siskiyous continued to offer all of its
courses online during Fall 2021 due to Covid. Thus, the faculty continued to strive to improve
the online presentation of their courses. During Spring 2022, the department offered two of the
Statistic courses face to face, while all the rest of the courses remained online. There is no
definite date when any of the rest of the math courses will be offered face to face.
PART 2: Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
Instructors are assessing outcomes, but do not know to whom this information should be
reported or how it should be reported. The department finished reducing the SLOs from 10 to 15
down to 3 to 5 per course. The changes were approved by the curriculum committee.
PART 3: Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
There were a few obstacles encountered by the mathematics department this year. Last
Spring semester, the department was given permission to use Pearson’s Mymathlab in which to
create online homework for the courses being offered online during Covid. However, by Fall of
this year, that decision was revoked, since College of the Siskiyous was unable to engage the
services of an accessibility expert to verify that Mymathlab meets accessibility standards. The
administration feels it is going to be impossible for the school of have any of the online math
programs vetted by the appropriate experts due to availability of experts as well as the cost, so
using Mymathlab, Hawkes, or other online homework problems will not be possible, at least for
the foreseeable future. As a result, the math department was given permission to use only
Myopenmath resources. However, Myopenmath program does not appear to be vetted for
accessibility either. Yet, the department needs an online homework program since the
department is offering the majority of its courses online. All of this presents a bit of a dilemma.
Another obstacle that instructors encounter with online teaching is editing closed captions
on any videos that they create for their students. This is a very time consuming process, so it
would be nice if the college had a few employees that could help with this process, so instructors
could dedicate more time to helping students.
Switching two of the Statistic classes back to face to face had a few obstacles. Offering
students enough area in the classroom so they could practice social distancing was a bit of a
challenge. Additionally, the extra air purifiers running in the room made it a little more difficult

-- 1 of 2 --

title: 21-22 Math Dept Ann Program Review (Part 2)
text:
for the students to hear the presentations and for instructors to hear students. Wearing masks
escalated this issue somewhat, too. It was also a new challenge for instructors who taught face to
face classes to “police” students to make sure they had checked in properly when they arrived at
school so they could obtain their wristband and to make sure they students observed the rules
about wearing face masks.
It was very helpful that the administration used some of the grant money it received due
to Covid to buy more and better technology for instructors. This made it easier to create lessons
and to maintain contact with online students.
Part 4: Describe program budgetary needs or implications.
The Mathematics department does not have a lot of money in its budget. This limits the
professional develop opportunities that instructors can attend. Also, if in the future student
enrollment increases, since there are only two full time instructors, there may be a need at some
point to hire another full time instructor.

-- 2 of 2 --
